text.title: Competitor Discovery and Mapping
text.summary: Competitor types, mapping process, URL-evidence requirement, and whitespace identification.

rule.statement: Cap the competitor list at 20 named entries with working URLs. Longer lists are hallucination magnets — nobody reads them and the data quality degrades past 20.
rule.rationale: Agents happily produce 40 competitor names when asked for a comprehensive list. Beyond 20, entries are increasingly invented, merged, or mischaracterized. A tight, URL-verified list is more actionable than an exhaustive fiction.

rule.statement: Reject any competitor entry without a working URL. Require URL plus a screenshot or page text as evidence that the product currently exists.
rule.rationale: Web-search agents invent product names, merge two products into one, or cite discontinued products as active competitors. URL verification is the minimum bar for a credible competitive landscape.

list.item: Direct: same solution, same problem, same customer — most visible, most cited
Indirect: different solution, same problem (e.g., Slack vs. Email for team communication)
Substitute: alternative approach entirely (e.g., outsource vs. software)
Potential: companies that could enter with their existing capabilities and distribution

rule.statement: Add a dedicated brainstorm step for substitute and potential competitors before locking the list. Agents routinely miss these two categories without an explicit prompt.
rule.rationale: Substitute and potential competitors drive the most strategic surprises (e.g., Notion entering Evernote's market, Cursor entering Copilot's space). They are invisible to competitive analysis that only scans existing product categories.

rule.statement: Make whitespace claims falsifiable: link to the specific competitor pricing pages and changelogs confirming the gap exists. A whitespace claim without evidence is a hypothesis, not a finding.
rule.rationale: Unfalsifiable whitespace claims drive product decisions based on assumptions that collapse on first customer call. Evidence-backed whitespace survives early customer validation.

text.reference: Competitor landscape table with direct, indirect, and whitespace sections
